2603.17171.title: Exploiting the English Grammar Profile for L2 grammatical analysis with LLMs
2603.17171.paper_summary: Evaluating the grammatical competence of second language (L2) learners is essential both for providing targeted feedback and for assessing proficiency. To achieve this, we propose a novel framework leveraging the English Grammar Profile (EGP), a taxonomy of grammatical constructs mapped to the proficiency levels of the Common European Framework of Reference (CEFR), to detect learners' attempts at grammatical constructs and classify them as successful or unsuccessful. This detection can then be used to provide fine-grained feedback. Moreover, the grammatical constructs are used as predictors of proficiency assessment by using automatically detected attempts as predictors of holistic CEFR proficiency. For the selection of grammatical constructs derived from the EGP, rule-based and LLM-based classifiers are compared. We show that LLMs outperform rule-based methods on semantically and pragmatically nuanced constructs, while rule-based approaches remain competitive for constructs that rely purely on morphological or syntactic features and do not require semantic interpretation. For proficiency assessment, we evaluate both rule-based and hybrid pipelines and show that a hybrid approach combining a rule-based pre-filter with an LLM consistently yields the strongest performance. Since our framework operates on pairs of original learner sentences and their corrected counterparts, we also evaluate a fully automated pipeline using automatic grammatical error correction. This pipeline closely approaches the performance of semi-automated systems based on manual corrections, particularly for the detection of successful attempts at grammatical constructs. Overall, our framework emphasises learners' successful attempts in addition to unsuccessful ones, enabling positive, formative feedback and providing actionable insights into grammatical development.
2603.17171.paper_summary_zh: 評估第二語言 (L2) 學習者的語法能力對於提供針對性的反饋和評估熟練度至關重要。<lb/>為了實現這一目標，我們提出了一個新穎的框架，利用英語語法概況 (EGP)，這是一種將語法結構映射到歐洲共同語言參考框架 (CEFR) 熟練度水平的分類法，以檢測學習者對語法結構的嘗試並將其分類為成功或不成功。<lb/>該檢測可以用來提供細緻的反饋。此外，這些語法結構被用作熟練度評估的預測因子，通過使用自動檢測的嘗試作為整體 CEFR 熟練度的預測因子。<lb/>在從 EGP 中選擇語法結構時，我們比較了基於規則和基於 LLM 的分類器。<lb/>我們展示了 LLM 在語義和語用微妙的結構上優於基於規則的方法，而基於規則的方法在純粹依賴形態或句法特徵且不需要語義解釋的結構上仍然具有競爭力。<lb/>在熟練度評估方面，我們評估了基於規則和混合管道，並顯示結合基於規則的預過濾器與 LLM 的混合方法始終產生最強的性能。<lb/>由於我們的框架在原始學習者句子及其修正對應句的對上運作，我們還評估了一個使用自動語法錯誤修正的完全自動化管道。<lb/>該管道的性能接近基於手動修正的半自動系統，特別是在檢測成功的語法結構嘗試方面。<lb/>總體而言，我們的框架強調學習者的成功嘗試，除了不成功的嘗試，從而促進積極的形成性反饋並提供有關語法發展的可行見解。
2603.17171.author: Stefano Bannò et.al.
2603.17171.authors: Stefano Bannò, Penny Karanasou, Kate Knill, Mark Gales
